item.title: Outlook for the euro area economy and monetary policy
item.speaker: Boris Vujčić

rating.summary: ECB Vice-President Vujčić presented the June 2026 Eurosystem staff macroeconomic projections at the Barclays-CEPR Monetary Policy Forum, covering the euro area growth and inflation outlook alongside alternative Middle East conflict scenarios. The baseline projects GDP growth of 0.8% in 2026 rising to 1.6% by 2028, with HICP inflation at 2.9% in 2026 declining toward 1.8% by 2027. The speech is largely a data and projection presentation without explicit forward guidance on policy rates.
rating.stance_rationale: The speech is predominantly a technical presentation of projections and scenarios with no explicit monetary policy guidance or directional signals, warranting a neutral score. Inflation is above target in 2026 but projected to decline, and growth is modest, leaving the overall policy signal balanced.
key_quotes: In all scenarios monetary and fiscal policies are the same as in the baseline, i.e. no policy change assumption.
